scenarios.title: Baseline (stylized)
scenarios.summary: No policy deltas relative to the model’s illustrative FY24-style baseline.
assumptions: Uses rounded public-order-of-magnitude receipts, outlays, and GDP from SOURCES.md.
Not a reproduction of any official CBO or JCT score.

scenarios.title: TCJA sunset (full, 2026)
scenarios.summary: Assumes key individual provisions tied to the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act phase out in 2026 with full depth in this toy model.
assumptions: Sunset depth maps to a stylized increase in individual income tax receipts; combined with a modest top-rate adjustment in the preset.
Year weighting is illustrative only—compare multiple sunset years in the API preset list.

scenarios.title: TCJA sunset (partial, 2026)
scenarios.summary: Same sunset year with partial provision expiration—smaller revenue effect than full sunset in the toy model.
assumptions: Partial depth uses an internal scalar (see API presets) and is not calibrated to a specific legislative text.

scenarios.title: Trump Platinum Card (illustrative)
scenarios.summary: Named scenario blending a luxury consumption fee, higher tariff proxy, lighter regulatory burden index, and a small individual rate tweak.
assumptions: Tariff and luxury fee levers are proxies for average effective changes, not product-level schedules.
Regulatory index is a scalar compliance-cost shifter, not a Federal Register rule count.
